_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
condition: Acute management of upper urinary tract trauma
procedure_name: Acute management of upper urinary tract trauma - conservative
source_files: Acute management of upper urinary tract trauma conservative_Medco.pdf
Acute management of upper urinary tract trauma conservative_PPD.CPD.pdf
clinical_indications: Conservative management is the best approach in stable patients
Angiography and selective embolization are the first line treatments
Conservative management is also advised in unilateral main arterial injuries, complete, blunt artery thrombosis, as well as in multiple-trauma patients

clinical_thresholds.note: Required clinical manifestation for proceeding with conservative management.

preauth.label: Clinical Notes including evaluation findings, indications for the procedure, and planned line of treatment

claim.label: Detailed Indoor case papers with treatment given

ppd_preauth.q: USG report suggestive for any injury?

cpd_claim.q: Detailed Discharge Summary submitted with the follow-up date mentioned?

common_queries: USG report not suggestive of any injury
Patient's systolic blood pressure not documented or documented >= 90 mm/hg without justification for conservative management
Clinical Notes missing or incomplete (lacking evaluation findings, indications, or planned line of treatment) at pre-authorization
USG abdomen report missing at pre-authorization
Detailed Indoor case papers or Detailed Procedure / Operative notes missing at claim submission
Detailed Discharge Summary missing or without follow-up date mentioned at claim submission
